title: Add a circle layer
text: First, set base map data.
code: library(toro) library(sf) #> Linking to GEOS 3.13.0, GDAL 3.8.5, PROJ 9.5.1; sf_use_s2() is TRUE  base_map <- map() |>   add_feature_server_source(     "https://services8.arcgis.com/AYGZtmUtpARUKBlB/arcgis/rest/services/Te_Reo_M%C4%81ori_Place_Names/FeatureServer/2/query?where=1=1&outFields=*&f=geojson",     "maori_maunga_data",     append_query_url = ""   )

title: Add a circle layer
code: base_map |>   add_circle_layer(     id = "maunga_circles",     source = "maori_moana_data",     hover_column = "name_mi",     popup_column = "name"   )

title: Add a circle layer
code: base_map |>   add_circle_layer(     id = "maunga_peaks",     source = "maori_maunga_data",     hover_column = "name_mi",     popup_column = "name",     paint = get_paint_options(       "circle",       options = list(         colour = get_column_group("type", c("volcano" = "#9d0208"), "#a3b18a"),         opacity = get_column_group("type", c("volcano" = 1), 0.8),         circle_radius = get_column_group("type", c("volcano" = 7), 4)       )     ),     layout = get_layout_options(       "circle",       options = list(         "circle-sort-key" = get_column_group("type", c("volcano" = 2), 1)       )     )   )

title: Add a symbol layer
text: First, set base map data.
code: library(toro) library(sf) #> Linking to GEOS 3.13.0, GDAL 3.8.5, PROJ 9.5.1; sf_use_s2() is TRUE  base_map <- map(   imageSources = list(     list(       id = "map_pin",       url = "https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/f/f2/678111-map-marker-512.png"     )   ) ) |>   add_feature_server_source(     "https://services8.arcgis.com/AYGZtmUtpARUKBlB/arcgis/rest/services/Te_Reo_M%C4%81ori_Place_Names/FeatureServer/0/query?where=1=1&outFields=*&f=geojson",     "maori_places_data",     append_query_url = ""   )

code: base_map |>   add_symbol_layer(     id = "places_text",     source = "maori_places_data",     hover_column = "type",     popup_column = "name",     can_cluster = FALSE,     filter = get_layer_filter("type == village"),     layout = toro::get_layout_options(       "symbol",       options = list(         icon_image = "map_pin",         icon_size = 0.05       )     )   )

title: Add a feature service source
text: ArcGIS Feature Service documentation

what: What is a feature service source?
title: Add a feature service source
text: Feature Service web service provides access geographic features attributes. toro, can add feature service data source map layers using add_feature_server_source function.

title: Add a feature service source
text: time, can simply provide URL feature service toro handle rest. default query parameters appended URL retrieve data GeoJSON format.
code: library(toro)  map() |>   add_feature_server_source(     "https://services1.arcgis.com/VwarAUbcaX64Jhub/arcgis/rest/services/World_Exclusive_Economic_Zones_Boundaries/FeatureServer",     "eez"   ) |>   add_line_layer(id = "eez_lines", source = "eez")

title: Add a feature service source
text: need control query parameters, can provide full query URL directly source_url argument set append_query_url empty string prevent appending default query parameters.
code: library(toro)  map() |>   add_feature_server_source(     "https://services8.arcgis.com/AYGZtmUtpARUKBlB/arcgis/rest/services/Te_Reo_M%C4%81ori_Place_Names/FeatureServer/4/query?where=1=1&outFields=*&f=geojson",     "maori_awa_data",     append_query_url = "" # We supply the full query URL here, so we set this to an empty string to avoid appending the default query parameters.   ) |>   add_line_layer(     id = "awa_lines",     source = "maori_awa_data",     hover_column = "name_mi",     popup_column = "name"   )

title: Add a GeoJSON source
code:
library(sf) #> Linking to GEOS 3.13.0, GDAL 3.8.5, PROJ 9.5.1; sf_use_s2() is TRUE library(dplyr) #> #> Attaching package: 'dplyr' #> The following objects are masked from 'package:stats': #> #> filter, lag #> The following objects are masked from 'package:base': #> #> intersect, setdiff, setequal, union data(quakes) quakes_data <- quakes |> mutate( description = paste( "
Location:", lat, ",", long, "
", "Magnitude:", mag, "
" ) ) |> st_as_sf(coords = c("long", "lat"), crs = 4326)

title: Add a GeoJSON source
text: two ways add GeoJSON source map:

title: Add a GeoJSON source
text: Using add_source() function, can add GeoJSON source passing sf object GeoJSON string directly data argument. sf objects automatically converted GeoJSON format added source. can use source adding layers map specifying source argument layer functions. can pass either mapor mapProxy object add_source().
code: library(toro)  map() |>   add_source("quakes_data", data = quakes_data) |>   add_circle_layer(     id = "quakes_circles",     source = "quakes_data",     hover_column = "description"   )

what: Adding the source directly in the layer function
title: Add a GeoJSON source
text: Alternatively, can add GeoJSON source directly within layer function passing sf object GeoJSON string source argument. case source ID automatically generated appending layer ID source-. example, add layer id = "my_layer" source sf object, source added map ID "source-my_layer". allows add source layer single step, without needing call add_source() separately.
code: library(toro)  map() |>   add_circle_layer(     id = "quakes_circles",     source = quakes_data,     hover_column = "description"   )

title: Add an image source
text: two ways add image source map:

title: Add an image source
text: Using add_image() function, can add image source passing image URL file path directly url argument. can pass either mapor mapProxy object add_image().
code: library(toro) library(sf) #> Linking to GEOS 3.13.0, GDAL 3.8.5, PROJ 9.5.1; sf_use_s2() is TRUE  epi_location <- data.frame(   lon = 174.82103783265418,   lat = -41.30961916092329 ) |>   st_as_sf(coords = c("lon", "lat"), crs = 4326)  map() |>   add_image(     image_id = "epi_logo",     image_url = "https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Epi-interactive-Ltd/toro/refs/heads/dev/vignettes/images/epi-logo.png"   ) |>   add_symbol_layer(     id = "epi",     source = epi_location,     layout = get_layout_options(       "symbol",       list(         icon_image = "epi_logo",         icon_size = 0.1       )     )   ) |>   set_bounds(epi_location, max_zoom = 13)

title: Clusters/Spiderfying
text: Note: Clusters currently work symbol(icon/pin) layers. Clusters disabled default, symbols coordinates always produce cluster (even can_cluster set false). Clicking cluster one two things: Zoom clusters pins Spiderfy pins cluster become visible
code: base_map  |>   add_symbol_layer(     id = "earthquakes",     source = quakes_data,     layout = toro::get_layout_options(       "symbol",       options = list(         icon_image = "map_pin",         icon_size = 0.05       )     ),     popup_column = "popup"   )

code: base_map  |>   add_symbol_layer(     id = "earthquakes",     source = quakes_data,     layout = toro::get_layout_options(       "symbol",       options = list(         icon_image = "map_pin",         icon_size = 0.05       )     ),     popup_column = "popup",     can_cluster = TRUE   )

title: Clusters/Spiderfying
text: can customise cluster appearance using clusterOptions. can set globally creating map specific layers adding layer.

title: Clusters/Spiderfying
text: clusterOptions.circleOptions changes apperance clusters circle. Available circle (paint/layout) properties can found .
code: map(   imageSources = list(     list(       id = "map_pin",       url = "https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/f/f2/678111-map-marker-512.png"     )   ),   # Setting the appearance globally for all clusters in the map   clusterOptions = list(     circleOptions = list(       paint = list(         "circle-color" = "#ebc642",         "circle-stroke-color" = "#fc038c",         "circle-radius" = 50       )     )   ) ) |>   add_symbol_layer(     id = "earthquakes",     source = quakes_data |> head(nrow(quakes_data) / 2),     layout = toro::get_layout_options(       "symbol",       options = list(         icon_image = "map_pin",         icon_size = 0.05       )     ),     popup_column = "popup",     can_cluster = TRUE   ) |>   add_symbol_layer(     id = "earthquakes_two",     source = quakes_data |> tail(nrow(quakes_data) / 2),     layout = toro::get_layout_options(       "symbol",       options = list(         icon_image = "map_pin",         icon_size = 0.05       )     ),     popup_column = "popup",     can_cluster = TRUE,     # Override the global cluster appearance for this layer only     clusterOptions = list(       circleOptions = list(         paint = list(           "circle-color" = "green",           "circle-stroke-color" = "orange",           "circle-radius" = 20         )       )     )   )

title: Clusters/Spiderfying
text: clusterOptions.textOptions changes apperance clusters text. Available text(symbol) (paint/layout) properties can found .
code: base_map  |>   add_symbol_layer(     id = "earthquakes",     source = quakes_data,     layout = toro::get_layout_options(       "symbol",       options = list(         icon_image = "map_pin",         icon_size = 0.05       )     ),     popup_column = "popup",     can_cluster = TRUE,     clusterOptions = list(       textOptions = list(         paint = list(           "text-color" = "red"         )       )     )   )

text: example advanced customising (see example).
code: base_map |>   add_symbol_layer(     id = "earthquakes",     source = quakes_data,     layout = toro::get_layout_options(       "symbol",       options = list(         icon_image = "map_pin",         icon_size = 0.05       )     ),     popup_column = "popup",     can_cluster = TRUE,     clusterOptions = list(       circleOptions = list(         paint = list(           "circle-color" = get_column_step_steps(             "point_count",             c(2, 5, 10),             c("red", "orange", "yellow", "green")           ),           "circle-stroke-color" = get_column_step_steps(             "point_count",             c(2, 5, 10),             c("red", "orange", "yellow", "green")           ),           "circle-radius" = get_column_step_steps(             "point_count",             c(2, 5, 10),             c(10, 20, 30, 40)           )         ),         layout = list(           "circle-sort-key" = get_column_step_steps(             "point_count",             c(2, 5, 10),             c(4, 3, 2, 1)           )         )       )     )   )

title: map-tiles

code: library(shiny) library(toro)   ui <- fluidPage(   toro::mapOutput("map"),   selectInput(     "tiles",     "Tiles",     choices = toro::get_tile_options(),     selected = "satellite"   ), )  server <- function(input, output, session) {   output$map <- toro::renderMap({     toro::map(style = "satellite", loadedTiles = toro::get_tile_options())   })    observe({     req(input$tiles)     toro::mapProxy("map") |>       set_tile_layer(input$tiles)   }) }  if (interactive()) {   shinyApp(ui, server) }

title: Get layout options for a specific layer type — get_layout_options
text: layer_type string indicating type layer (e.g., "fill", "circle", "line"). options list additional options customize layout properties. See Maplibre docs options. line_cap: list options line cap, layer_type "line". Default "round". line_join: list options line join, layer_type "line". Default "round". icon_image: name icon image use symbol layers, layer_type "symbol". Default "toro-pin". icon_size: size icon symbol layers, layer_type "symbol". Default 1. icon_anchor: anchor point icon symbol layers, layer_type "symbol". Default "bottom". text_anchor: anchor point text symbol layers, layer_type "symbol". Default "center". icon_offset: offset icon symbol layers, layer_type "symbol". Default list(0, 0). icon_allow_overlap: Whether allow icons overlap symbol layers, layer_type "symbol". Default TRUE. text_allow_overlap: Whether allow text overlap symbol layers, layer_type "symbol". Default TRUE. icon_rotate: rotation angle icons symbol layers, layer_type "symbol". Default 0. icon_flip_horizontal: Whether flip icons horizontally symbol layers, layer_type "symbol". Default FALSE. Note uses icon-flip-horizontal property Maplibre, may supported icons. text_font: font text symbol layers, layer_type "symbol". Default "Open Sans Regular". text_field: text field symbol layers, layer_type "symbol". Default NULL, means text shown. can set column value using get_column functions show text data. text_size: size text symbol layers, layer_type "symbol". Default 12. can also provide layout options found Maplibre docs specific layer type, included returned list.

Optional. height height widget. Optional. session Shiny session object. Default current session. ... Additional options customize map. minZoom: Minimum zoom level (0-24). Full options: c("natgeo", "satellite", "topo", "terrain", "streets", "shaded", "lightgrey"). Default c("lightgrey", "satellite"). initialTileLayer: tile layer use map first loaded. Default NULL (first layer loadedTiles). backgroundColour: background colour map. Default "#D0CFD4". enable3D: Whether enable 3D dragging/view. Default FALSE. initialLoadedLayers: character vector layer ids loaded initial map spinner hidden. Default NULL. spinnerWhileBusy: Whether show spinner map busy (e.g. loading tiles). Default FALSE. busyLoaderBgColour: background colour busy loader. Default "rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.2)". busyLoaderColour: colour busy loader. Default "white". initialLoaderBgColour: background colour initial loader. Default "white". initialLoaderColour: colour initial loader. Default "black". clusterOptions: list options clustering, can_cluster TRUE. See cluster vignette details available options. attributionPosition: position attribution control. Default "bottom-right".
